recalls.Description: MPINOI has issued a recall for its adult portable bed rails due to significant safety concerns. These white metal bed rails, measuring approximately 15 inches wide and 26 inches tall, feature an extendable handle with gray padding and a black storage mesh pocket, which is marked with the "MPINOI" logo. The product poses a serious risk of entrapment and potential asphyxiation, as users may become trapped between the bed rail and the mattress. Furthermore, these rails lack the necessary hazard warning labels, violating safety standards.
recalls.Title: Recall of MPINOI Adult Portable Bed Rails Due to Serious Entrapment and Asphyxiation Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out via email at MPINOIproductrecall@outlook.com.

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risk due to non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the recalled bed rails and contact Bioenrrty for a full refund. Consumers must destroy the bed rails by cutting the black safety strap in half, marking the rails with "RECALLED" using a permanent marker, and sending a photo of the destroyed product to MPINOIproductrecall@outlook.com.

recalls.Description:
Sunnyyes has issued a recall for its LED mini lights, which feature 13 color options and include two CR2032 lithium coin batteries in each of the 10 lights. Additionally, the lights come with two remote controls, each powered by a CR2025 coin lithium battery. These lights measure 1.18 inches in diameter and pose a significant hazard to children due to easily accessible batteries. The design fails to meet safety standards, as the remote control battery compartments can be opened without tools, and essential warning labels are missing from the packaging.

Consumers are urged to stop using the affected LED lights immediately and ensure they are stored out of reach of children. To obtain a full refund, customers must disassemble the lights and submerge all parts in water, then send a photo of the submerged components to the provided email address. It's important to handle button cell and coin batteries with care and dispose of them according to local hazardous waste guidelines.
recalls.Title: Recall of Sunnyyes LED Mini Lights Due to Serious Battery Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Contact Sunnyyes via email at usa@sunnyyes.com.

recalls.Description: Vive Health is recalling specific models of their bed rails due to a significant risk of entrapment and asphyxiation, which has led to two reported deaths. The affected products include the Vive Health Compact Bed Rail (model LVA2009SLV), Bed Rail Collection V (model LVA2097SLV), and units of the Vive Health Bed Rail model LVA1024 purchased on or before August 21, 2023. These bed rails are typically made from silver or white metal tubing and feature a black foam rubber grip handle. Some recalled units may lack model numbers or brand labels.

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Vive Health at 800-487-3808 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recalls@vivehealth.com. Retailers.Name: Vive Health distributed approximately 122,000 units of these bed rails through medical supply stores nationwide and online at www.vivehealth.com, Amazon, and other online medical supply retailers. recalls.Description: 4our Kiddies has issued a recall for their plastic tip restraint kits, commonly known as furniture straps, due to a significant risk of tip-over incidents. These kits include two white plastic brackets, a zip tie, various screws, and drywall anchors. Over time, the plastic components may deteriorate, leading to potential furniture tipping when interacted with, posing serious injury or death risks, especially to children and the elderly. CPSC testing has confirmed that these kits do not meet safety standards for tip restraints. For further safety information, visit CPSC's Anchor It! website at www.anchorit.gov/.
recalls.Title: 4our Kiddies Tip Restraint Kits Recalled Due to Serious Tip-Over Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to 4our Kiddies via email at furniturestraps-recall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled 4our Kiddies Plastic Tip Restraint

Injuries.Name: CPSC is aware of at least 150 incidents where consumers reported that the defective plastic tip restraint kits broke, including three reported tip-over incidents.

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Amazon.com from June 2019 to January 2026, priced between $6 and $21.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen City Donglin E-Commerce Co., Ltd d/b/a 4our Kiddies, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment due to potential breakage or degradation of the plastic components.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled kits and contact 4our Kiddies for a complimentary stainless steel replacement kit. Keep children away from the furniture until the new kit is installed, and dispose of the recalled kit safely.

recalls.metaDescription: 4our Kiddies recalls tip restraint kits due to tip-over hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement and ensure safety for your home.

recalls.Description:
Prismatic 3D Prints is recalling its Book Nooks with Lights due to a serious risk of battery ingestion that could lead to severe injuries or even death. These decorative items are designed to fit between books on a shelf and come in various designs such as Fantasy Window and Gothic Cathedral Window. They are available in multiple frame and window color options, including antique gold and purple. A notable feature is a circular recess at the back for storing spare CR2032 coin cell batteries, which poses a safety concern for children.

The recalled book nooks do not comply with mandatory safety standards for products containing coin batteries. The spare lithium coin battery is easily accessible and lacks child-resistant packaging, failing to meet the requirements set forth by Reese's Law. Ingestion of these batteries can result in severe internal injuries and chemical burns, making this recall critical for consumer safety.

recalls.Description:
KingPavonini has issued a recall for six models of their adult portable bed rails due to safety concerns that could lead to serious injury or death. These bed rails, identifiable by model numbers BC-CBJ-X, BC-CBJ, BC-LDFS-X, LDFS-SSK, 04-BC-LDFS, and BC-LDFS, feature white or black tubing and rounded black foam rubber handle grips. recalls.Description: Cranach Hardware is recalling its plastic tip restraint kits, also known as furniture straps, due to safety concerns. These kits include two white plastic brackets for securing furniture to the wall, a plastic cable zip tie, screws, and drywall anchors. At Home is recalling its Tie Dye Fur Toy Trunk Storage Chests, available in two sizes. The large trunk measures approximately 32-½" x 16-½" x 21", while the small trunk measures around 27" x 13-½" x 16-½". These chests are constructed from MDF wood and feature a vibrant multi-color tie dye faux fur covering. recalls.Description:
Dania Furniture has recalled the Hayden bookcase, which features six storage cubbies and is constructed from brown wood with three sliding white doors. The dimensions of the bookcase are 35.5 inches wide, 16 inches deep, and 73 inches tall. Each unit is labeled on the back with the product name and SKU number LB2225/A. This product poses significant dangers if not properly anchored to the wall, leading to potential tip-over incidents that can cause severe injury or even death to children.

Consumers are urged to stop using the bookcase immediately if it is not secured to a wall. It should be stored in an area inaccessible to children. Dania Furniture is offering a free in-home installation of a tip-over restraint kit. Should consumers prefer a refund or if installation services cannot be completed, Dania Furniture will facilitate a full refund upon the return of the bookcase and arrange for its pickup or disposal. The company is proactively reaching out to all affected purchasers.

The Classic quilts are available in various colors including white, natural, ticking stripe, blue, and gray, and come in sizes such as twin, full/queen, and king/California king. The quilted euro shams are offered in white, natural, and ticking stripe, while king and standard shams are available in white, natural, blue, gray, and ticking stripe. Additionally, baby quilts are sold in white, natural, and charcoal ticking print.
recalls.Title: Red Land Cotton Recalls Quilts and Shams Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Red Land Cotton at 877-478-3467 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email customer service at info@redlandcotton.com. Consumers can also visit www.redlandcotton.com/pages/voluntary-quilt-recall or check the "Voluntary Recall" tab on their website for more details.

recalls.Description: Essential Medical Supply has reannounced a recall of several models of adult portable bed rails due to serious entrapment and asphyxia hazards. The affected models include the Hand Bed Rail (model P1410), Hand Bed Rail with Pouch (model P1410-P), Hand Bed Rail with Floor Support (model P1411), and Hand Bed Rail with Floor Support with Pouch (model P1411-P). These rails, identifiable by the brand name and model number on the grip handle, are constructed of chrome or white metal tubing and feature black padding. Only models without an 'R' at the end of the model number are part of this recall.
recalls.Title: Essential Medical Supply Recalls Adult Portable Bed Rails Due to Entrapment and Asphyxia Risks; Additional Deaths Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers should contact Essential Medical Supply at 800-826-8423 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit essentialmedicalsupply.com and click on "Important Safety Notice" for more information.

Injuries.Name: In December 2022, an 86-year-old man died at an assisted living facility in Connecticut, and in August 2023, a 99-year-old man died at his home in California. CPSC previously announced the recall on December 22, 2021, following the entrapment death of an 86-year-old man in California. He died at his home in December 2012, reportedly from the Endurance Hand Bed Rail (model P1410).

Importers.Name: Essential Medical Supply Inc., of Orlando, Florida

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and asphyxiation risk when users become trapped between the bed rail and mattress.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bed rails immediately. A pro-rated refund will be provided for those purchased after November 1, 2015.

recalls.metaDescription: Essential Medical Supply recalls adult bed rails due to serious safety hazards, urging consumers to stop use and seek refunds.

recalls.Description:
Alliance4Safety, in collaboration with 31 furniture companies, is recalling plastic tip-over restraint kits designed for New Age furniture. These kits were distributed with clothing storage units manufactured in Vietnam. The kits include a plastic zip-tie, two brackets, and screws, and are identifiable by packaging that features white background with black text, stating 'Manufactured by New Age Industries.' The date of manufacture is stamped or stickered on the unit as November 2019 or later.

The recall is prompted by safety concerns, as the plastic zip-tie may become brittle or break, potentially allowing the anchored clothing storage unit to detach during a tip-over, posing serious risks of entrapment or injury to children. Consumers are urged to check the manufacturing date and take necessary actions to ensure their children's safety.
recalls.Title: Recall of New Age Furniture Tip-over Restraint Kits by Alliance4Safety and 31 Furniture Brands Due to Child Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Alliance4Safety toll-free at 855-416-7370 anytime or visit www.alliance4safety.org/new-age-recall or www.alliance4safety.org and select 'New Age Recall Information.'

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury from the outer case or cabinet breaking and falling when opening the bed, and potential tip-over and entrapment hazards if not anchored to the wall.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled Xtrabeds immediately and contact J Grubb Designs to arrange a free home repair by a technician. Alternatively, a full refund is available for those who choose not to receive the repair. J Grubb Designs will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert for Windsor Queen Xtrabed due to injury and tip-over hazards. Contact J Grubb Designs for repairs or refunds.

recalls.Description: Adven Group is recalling all sizes of the Nap Queen 12-inch Maxima Hybrid Mattress due to a serious fire hazard. This includes models NQIH12KK, NQIH12QQ, NQIH12FF, NQIH12TT, and NQIH12TX. The mattress features a single-sided design with white and gray-dotted fabric, a dark gray mesh stripe, and a quilted pattern. The model name 'Maxima' and the importer's details are labeled on the cover. These mattresses were sold compressed in a box.
recalls.Title: Fire Hazard Recall of Nap Queen Maxima Hybrid Mattresses by Adven Group
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Nap Queen at 866-387-6239 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://sleepqueens.myshopify.com/pages/napqueen-maxima-12-twin-size-mattress-recall or www.napqueensleep.com and click on the 'Product Recall' banner for more information.

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet federal flammability standards, creating a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the mattresses and contact Nap Queen for a free fitted cover to ensure compliance with federal standards.

recalls.Description: Foiresoft Basic Zebra Roller Blinds are being recalled due to serious safety concerns. These blinds, available in various colors and sizes ranging from 10 to 110 inches in width and 64 inches in height, feature a label with 'Foiresoft' on the headrail. The design includes looped operating cords that can pose a strangulation risk to young children.
recalls.Title: Foiresoft Zebra Roller Blinds Recalled for Strangulation Risks; Exclusively Sold on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers should reach out to Softfunch Inc. at 800-518-4391, available from 8 a.m. to 12 p.m. ET Monday through Thursday, or via email at recall.foiresoft@gmail.com. Additional details can be found online at https://www.foiresoft.co.kr/recall.

Retailers.Name: Sold exclusively on Amazon.com from December 2022 to July 2023, priced between $50 and $175.

Importers.Name: Softfunch, of South Korea

Hazards.Name: Strangulation hazard due to looped operating cords

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the blinds immediately and contact Softfunch Inc. for a full refund. Proof of destruction is required, including a photo of the cut product with cut cords. Dispose of the blinds in your trash. Softfunch will directly contact all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Foiresoft Zebra Roller Blinds recalled due to strangulation risks. Contact Softfunch for a refund and disposal instructions.
